endpoints.name: POST /v1/organizations
endpoints.description: Creates an Apigee organization. See [Create an Apigee organization](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/get-started/create-org).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{instance}:reportStatus
endpoints.description: Reports the latest status for a runtime instance.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{INSTANCE}} with a real instance.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Gets a category on the portal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Updates a shared flow revision. This operation is only allowed on revisions which have never been deployed. After deployment a revision becomes immutable, even if it becomes undeployed. The payload is a ZIP-formatted shared flow. Content type must be either multipart/form-data or application/octet-stream.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: PUT /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Update an existing custom report definition
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Deletes a category from the portal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: PATCH /v1/{name}
endpoints.description: Updates a category on the portal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}/attributes
endpoints.description: Updates attributes for a developer app. This API replaces the current attributes with those specified in the request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}/certificate
endpoints.description: Gets the certificate from an alias in PEM-encoded form.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}/csr
endpoints.description: Generates a PKCS #10 Certificate Signing Request for the private key in an alias.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1/{name}/data
endpoints.description: Deletes the data from a debug session. This does not cancel the debug session or prevent further data from being collected if the session is still active in runtime pods.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}/deployments
endpoints.description: Gets the deployment of a shared flow revision and actual state reported by runtime pods.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}/deployments
endpoints.description: Deploys a revision of a shared flow. If another revision of the same shared flow is currently deployed, set the `override` parameter to `true` to have this revision replace the currently deployed revision. You cannot use a shared flow until it has been deployed to an environment. For a request path `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}/sharedflows/{sf}/revisions/{rev}/deployments`, two permissions are required: * `apigee.deployments.create` on the resource `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}` * `apigee.sharedflowrevisions.deploy` on the resource `organizations/{org}/sharedflows/{sf}/revisions/{rev}`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1/{name}/deployments
endpoints.description: Undeploys a shared flow revision from an environment. For a request path `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}/sharedflows/{sf}/revisions/{rev}/deployments`, two permissions are required: * `apigee.deployments.delete` on the resource `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}` * `apigee.sharedflowrevisions.undeploy` on the resource `organizations/{org}/sharedflows/{sf}/revisions/{rev}`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}/deployments:generateDeployChangeReport
endpoints.description: Generates a report for a dry run analysis of a DeployApiProxy request without committing the deployment. In addition to the standard validations performed when adding deployments, additional analysis will be done to detect possible traffic routing changes that would result from this deployment being created. Any potential routing conflicts or unsafe changes will be reported in the response. This routing analysis is not performed for a non-dry-run DeployApiProxy request. For a request path `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}/apis/{api}/revisions/{rev}/deployments:generateDeployChangeReport`, two permissions are required: * `apigee.deployments.create` on the resource `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}` * `apigee.proxyrevisions.deploy` on the resource `organizations/{org}/apis/{api}/revisions/{rev}`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}/deployments:generateUndeployChangeReport
endpoints.description: Generates a report for a dry run analysis of an UndeployApiProxy request without committing the undeploy. In addition to the standard validations performed when removing deployments, additional analysis will be done to detect possible traffic routing changes that would result from this deployment being removed. Any potential routing conflicts or unsafe changes will be reported in the response. This routing analysis is not performed for a non-dry-run UndeployApiProxy request. For a request path `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}/apis/{api}/revisions/{rev}/deployments:generateUndeployChangeReport`, two permissions are required: * `apigee.deployments.delete` on the resource `organizations/{org}/environments/{env}` * `apigee.proxyrevisions.undeploy` on the resource `organizations/{org}/apis/{api}/revisions/{rev}`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}/operations
endpoints.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:activate
endpoints.description: Activates the NAT address. The Apigee instance can now use this for Internet egress traffic. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:adjust
endpoints.description: Adjust the prepaid balance for the developer. This API will be used in scenarios where the developer has been under-charged or over-charged.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:credit
endpoints.description: Credits the account balance for the developer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:expire
endpoints.description: Expires an API product subscription immediately.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:generateDownloadUrl
endpoints.description: Generates a signed URL for downloading the original zip file used to create an Archive Deployment. The URL is only valid for a limited period and should be used within minutes after generation. Each call returns a new upload URL.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}:getProjectMapping
endpoints.description: Gets the project ID and region for an Apigee organization.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:getSyncAuthorization
endpoints.description: Lists the service accounts with the permissions required to allow the Synchronizer to download environment data from the control plane. An ETag is returned in the response to `getSyncAuthorization`. Pass that ETag when calling [setSyncAuthorization](setSyncAuthorization) to ensure that you are updating the correct version. If you don't pass the ETag in the call to `setSyncAuthorization`, then the existing authorization is overwritten indiscriminately. For more information, see [Configure the Synchronizer](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/hybrid/latest/synchronizer-access). **Note**: Available to Apigee hybrid only.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{name}:listRevisions
endpoints.description: ListSecurityProfileRevisions lists all the revisions of the security profile.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{name}:setSyncAuthorization
endpoints.description: Sets the permissions required to allow the Synchronizer to download environment data from the control plane. You must call this API to enable proper functioning of hybrid. Pass the ETag when calling `setSyncAuthorization` to ensure that you are updating the correct version. To get an ETag, call [getSyncAuthorization](getSyncAuthorization). If you don't pass the ETag in the call to `setSyncAuthorization`, then the existing authorization is overwritten indiscriminately. For more information, see [Configure the Synchronizer](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/hybrid/latest/synchronizer-access). **Note**: Available to Apigee hybrid only.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{orgenv}/securityStats:queryTabularStats
endpoints.description: Retrieve security statistics as tabular rows.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ORGENV}} with a real orgenv.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{orgenv}/securityStats:queryTimeSeriesStats
endpoints.description: Retrieve security statistics as a collection of time series.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ORGENV}} with a real orgenv.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{org}:setAddons
endpoints.description: Configures the add-ons for the Apigee organization. The existing add-on configuration will be fully replaced.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ORG}} with a real org.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}
endpoints.description: Lists the Apigee organizations and associated Google Cloud projects that you have permission to access. See [Understanding organizations](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/fundamentals/organization-structure).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/aliases
endpoints.description: Creates an alias from a key/certificate pair. The structure of the request is controlled by the `format` query parameter: - `keycertfile` - Separate PEM-encoded key and certificate files are uploaded. Set `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` and include the `keyFile`, `certFile`, and `password` (if keys are encrypted) fields in the request body. If uploading to a truststore, omit `keyFile`. - `pkcs12` - A PKCS12 file is uploaded. Set `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`, provide the file in the `file` field, and include the `password` field if the file is encrypted in the request body. - `selfsignedcert` - A new private key and certificate are generated. Set `Content-Type: application/json` and include CertificateGenerationSpec in the request body.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/analytics/datastores
endpoints.description: List Datastores
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/analytics/datastores
endpoints.description: Create a Datastore for an org
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/analytics/datastores:test
endpoints.description: Test if Datastore configuration is correct. This includes checking if credentials provided by customer have required permissions in target destination storage
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/analytics/exports
endpoints.description: Lists the details and status of all analytics export jobs belonging to the parent organization and environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/analytics/exports
endpoints.description: Submit a data export job to be processed in the background. If the request is successful, the API returns a 201 status, a URI that can be used to retrieve the status of the export job, and the `state` value of "enqueued".
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/apicategories
endpoints.description: Lists the categories on the portal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/apicategories
endpoints.description: Creates a new category on the portal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/apiproducts
endpoints.description: Lists all API product names for an organization. Filter the list by passing an `attributename` and `attibutevalue`. The maximum number of API products returned is 1000. You can paginate the list of API products returned using the `startKey` and `count` query parameters.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/apiproducts
endpoints.description: Creates an API product in an organization. You create API products after you have proxied backend services using API proxies. An API product is a collection of API resources combined with quota settings and metadata that you can use to deliver customized and productized API bundles to your developer community. This metadata can include: - Scope - Environments - API proxies - Extensible profile API products enable you repackage APIs on the fly, without having to do any additional coding or configuration. Apigee recommends that you start with a simple API product including only required elements. You then provision credentials to apps to enable them to start testing your APIs. After you have authentication and authorization working against a simple API product, you can iterate to create finer-grained API products, defining different sets of API resources for each API product. **WARNING:** - If you don't specify an API proxy in the request body, *any* app associated with the product can make calls to *any* API in your entire organization. - If you don't specify an environment in the request body, the product allows access to all environments. For more information, see What is an API product?
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/apis
endpoints.description: Lists the names of all API proxies in an organization. The names returned correspond to the names defined in the configuration files for each API proxy.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/apis
endpoints.description: Creates an API proxy. The API proxy created will not be accessible at runtime until it is deployed to an environment. Create a new API proxy by setting the `name` query parameter to the name of the API proxy. Import an API proxy configuration bundle stored in zip format on your local machine to your organization by doing the following: * Set the `name` query parameter to the name of the API proxy. * Set the `action` query parameter to `import`. * Set the `Content-Type` header to `multipart/form-data`. * Pass as a file the name of API proxy configuration bundle stored in zip format on your local machine using the `file` form field. **Note**: To validate the API proxy configuration bundle only without importing it, set the `action` query parameter to `validate`. When importing an API proxy configuration bundle, if the API proxy does not exist, it will be created. If the API proxy exists, then a new revision is created. Invalid API proxy configurations are rejected, and a list of validation errors is returned to the client.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/apps
endpoints.description: Lists all apps created by a developer in an Apigee organization. Optionally, you can request an expanded view of the developer apps. A maximum of 100 developer apps are returned per API call. You can paginate the list of deveoper apps returned using the `startKey` and `count` query parameters.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/apps
endpoints.description: Creates an app associated with a developer. This API associates the developer app with the specified API product and auto-generates an API key for the app to use in calls to API proxies inside that API product. The `name` is the unique ID of the app that you can use in API calls. The `DisplayName` (set as an attribute) appears in the UI. If you don't set the `DisplayName` attribute, the `name` appears in the UI.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/archiveDeployments
endpoints.description: Lists the ArchiveDeployments in the specified Environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/archiveDeployments
endpoints.description: Creates a new ArchiveDeployment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/archiveDeployments:generateUploadUrl
endpoints.description: Generates a signed URL for uploading an Archive zip file to Google Cloud Storage. Once the upload is complete, the signed URL should be passed to CreateArchiveDeployment. When uploading to the generated signed URL, please follow these restrictions: * Source file type should be a zip file. * Source file size should not exceed 1GB limit. * No credentials should be attached - the signed URLs provide access to the target bucket using internal service identity; if credentials were attached, the identity from the credentials would be used, but that identity does not have permissions to upload files to the URL. When making a HTTP PUT request, these two headers need to be specified: * `content-type: application/zip` * `x-goog-content-length-range: 0,1073741824` And this header SHOULD NOT be specified: * `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/attachments
endpoints.description: Lists all attachments to an instance. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/attachments
endpoints.description: Creates a new attachment of an environment to an instance. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/attributes
endpoints.description: Returns a list of all developer attributes.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/attributes
endpoints.description: Updates developer attributes. This API replaces the existing attributes with those specified in the request. Add new attributes, and include or exclude any existing attributes that you want to retain or remove, respectively. The custom attribute limit is 18. **Note**: OAuth access tokens and Key Management Service (KMS) entities (apps, developers, and API products) are cached for 180 seconds (default). Any custom attributes associated with these entities are cached for at least 180 seconds after the entity is accessed at runtime. Therefore, an `ExpiresIn` element on the OAuthV2 policy won't be able to expire an access token in less than 180 seconds.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/canaryevaluations
endpoints.description: Creates a new canary evaluation for an organization.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/datacollectors
endpoints.description: Lists all data collectors.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/datacollectors
endpoints.description: Creates a new data collector.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/debugsessions
endpoints.description: Lists debug sessions that are currently active in the given API Proxy revision.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/debugsessions
endpoints.description: Creates a debug session for a deployed API Proxy revision.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/deployments
endpoints.description: Lists all deployments of a shared flow revision.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/developers
endpoints.description: Lists all developers in an organization by email address. By default, the response does not include company developers. Set the `includeCompany` query parameter to `true` to include company developers. **Note**: A maximum of 1000 developers are returned in the response. You paginate the list of developers returned using the `startKey` and `count` query parameters.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/developers
endpoints.description: Creates a developer. Once created, the developer can register an app and obtain an API key. At creation time, a developer is set as `active`. To change the developer status, use the SetDeveloperStatus API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/endpointAttachments
endpoints.description: Lists the endpoint attachments in an organization.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/endpointAttachments
endpoints.description: Creates an endpoint attachment. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/entries
endpoints.description: Lists key value entries for key values maps scoped to an organization, environment, or API proxy. **Note**: Supported for Apigee hybrid 1.8.x and higher.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/entries
endpoints.description: Creates key value entries in a key value map scoped to an organization, environment, or API proxy. **Note**: Supported for Apigee hybrid 1.8.x and higher.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/envgroups
endpoints.description: Lists all environment groups.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/envgroups
endpoints.description: Creates a new environment group.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/hostQueries
endpoints.description: Return a list of Asynchronous Queries at host level.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/hostQueries
endpoints.description: Submit a query at host level to be processed in the background. If the submission of the query succeeds, the API returns a 201 status and an ID that refer to the query. In addition to the HTTP status 201, the `state` of "enqueued" means that the request succeeded.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/hostSecurityReports
endpoints.description: Return a list of Security Reports at host level.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/hostSecurityReports
endpoints.description: Submit a query at host level to be processed in the background. If the submission of the query succeeds, the API returns a 201 status and an ID that refer to the query. In addition to the HTTP status 201, the `state` of "enqueued" means that the request succeeded.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/instances
endpoints.description: Lists all Apigee runtime instances for the organization. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/instances
endpoints.description: Creates an Apigee runtime instance. The instance is accessible from the authorized network configured on the organization. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/keys
endpoints.description: Creates a custom consumer key and secret for a developer app. This is particularly useful if you want to migrate existing consumer keys and secrets to Apigee from another system. Consumer keys and secrets can contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens. No other special characters are allowed. To avoid service disruptions, a consumer key and secret should not exceed 2 KBs each. **Note**: When creating the consumer key and secret, an association to API products will not be made. Therefore, you should not specify the associated API products in your request. Instead, use the UpdateDeveloperAppKey API to make the association after the consumer key and secret are created. If a consumer key and secret already exist, you can keep them or delete them using the DeleteDeveloperAppKey API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/keys/create
endpoints.description: Creates a custom consumer key and secret for a developer app. This is particularly useful if you want to migrate existing consumer keys and secrets to Apigee from another system. Consumer keys and secrets can contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens. No other special characters are allowed. To avoid service disruptions, a consumer key and secret should not exceed 2 KBs each. **Note**: When creating the consumer key and secret, an association to API products will not be made. Therefore, you should not specify the associated API products in your request. Instead, use the UpdateDeveloperAppKey API to make the association after the consumer key and secret are created. If a consumer key and secret already exist, you can keep them or delete them using the DeleteDeveloperAppKey API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/keystores
endpoints.description: Creates a keystore or truststore. - Keystore: Contains certificates and their associated keys. - Truststore: Contains trusted certificates used to validate a server's certificate. These certificates are typically self-signed certificates or certificates that are not signed by a trusted CA.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/keyvaluemaps
endpoints.description: Creates a key value map in an organization.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/natAddresses
endpoints.description: Lists the NAT addresses for an Apigee instance. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/natAddresses
endpoints.description: Creates a NAT address. The address is created in the RESERVED state and a static external IP address will be provisioned. At this time, the instance will not use this IP address for Internet egress traffic. The address can be activated for use once any required firewall IP whitelisting has been completed. **Note:** Not supported for Apigee hybrid.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/overrides
endpoints.description: Lists all of the distributed trace configuration overrides in an environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/overrides
endpoints.description: Creates a trace configuration override. The response contains a system-generated UUID, that can be used to view, update, or delete the configuration override. Use the List API to view the existing trace configuration overrides.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/queries
endpoints.description: Return a list of Asynchronous Queries
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/queries
endpoints.description: Submit a query to be processed in the background. If the submission of the query succeeds, the API returns a 201 status and an ID that refer to the query. In addition to the HTTP status 201, the `state` of "enqueued" means that the request succeeded.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/rateplans
endpoints.description: Lists all the rate plans for an API product.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/rateplans
endpoints.description: Create a rate plan that is associated with an API product in an organization. Using rate plans, API product owners can monetize their API products by configuring one or more of the following: - Billing frequency - Initial setup fees for using an API product - Payment funding model (postpaid only) - Fixed recurring or consumption-based charges for using an API product - Revenue sharing with developer partners An API product can have multiple rate plans associated with it but *only one* rate plan can be active at any point of time. **Note: From the developer's perspective, they purchase API products not rate plans.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/references
endpoints.description: Creates a Reference in the specified environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/reports
endpoints.description: Return a list of Custom Reports
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/reports
endpoints.description: Creates a Custom Report for an Organization. A Custom Report provides Apigee Customers to create custom dashboards in addition to the standard dashboards which are provided. The Custom Report in its simplest form contains specifications about metrics, dimensions and filters. It is important to note that the custom report by itself does not provide an executable entity. The Edge UI converts the custom report definition into an analytics query and displays the result in a chart.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles
endpoints.description: Lists all resource files, optionally filtering by type. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles
endpoints.description: Creates a resource file. Specify the `Content-Type` as `application/octet-stream` or `multipart/form-data`. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles/{type}
endpoints.description: Lists all resource files, optionally filtering by type. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent. Replace {{TYPE}} with a real type.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles/{type}/{name}
endpoints.description: Gets the contents of a resource file. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent. Replace {{TYPE}} with a real type. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: PUT /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles/{type}/{name}
endpoints.description: Updates a resource file. Specify the `Content-Type` as `application/octet-stream` or `multipart/form-data`. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent. Replace {{TYPE}} with a real type. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: DELETE /v1/{parent}/resourcefiles/{type}/{name}
endpoints.description: Deletes a resource file. For more information about resource files, see [Resource files](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/develop/resource-files).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent. Replace {{TYPE}} with a real type. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/securityIncidents
endpoints.description: ListSecurityIncidents lists all the security incident associated with the environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/securityProfiles
endpoints.description: ListSecurityProfiles lists all the security profiles associated with the org including attached and unattached profiles.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/securityReports
endpoints.description: Return a list of Security Reports
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/securityReports
endpoints.description: Submit a report request to be processed in the background. If the submission succeeds, the API returns a 200 status and an ID that refer to the report request. In addition to the HTTP status 200, the `state` of "enqueued" means that the request succeeded.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/sharedflows
endpoints.description: Lists all shared flows in the organization.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/sharedflows
endpoints.description: Uploads a ZIP-formatted shared flow configuration bundle to an organization. If the shared flow already exists, this creates a new revision of it. If the shared flow does not exist, this creates it. Once imported, the shared flow revision must be deployed before it can be accessed at runtime. The size limit of a shared flow bundle is 15 MB.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{parent}/subscriptions
endpoints.description: Lists all API product subscriptions for a developer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/subscriptions
endpoints.description: Creates a subscription to an API product.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}/targetservers
endpoints.description: Creates a TargetServer in the specified environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}:subscribe
endpoints.description: Creates a subscription for the environment's Pub/Sub topic. The server will assign a random name for this subscription. The "name" and "push_config" must *not* be specified.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{parent}:unsubscribe
endpoints.description: Deletes a subscription for the environment's Pub/Sub topic.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PARENT}} with a real parent.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{profileEnvironment}:computeEnvironmentScores
endpoints.description: ComputeEnvironmentScores calculates scores for requested time range for the specified security profile and environment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROFILEENVIRONMENT}} with a real profileenvironment.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{project}:provisionOrganization
endpoints.description: Provisions a new Apigee organization with a functioning runtime. This is the standard way to create trial organizations for a free Apigee trial.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{PROJECT}} with a real project.

endpoints.name: GET /v1/{resource}:getIamPolicy
endpoints.description: Gets the IAM policy on an environment. For more information, see [Manage users, roles, and permissions using the API](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/system-administration/manage-users-roles). You must have the `apigee.environments.getIamPolicy` permission to call this API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{RESOURCE}} with a real resource.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{resource}:setIamPolicy
endpoints.description: Sets the IAM policy on an environment, if the policy already exists it will be replaced. For more information, see [Manage users, roles, and permissions using the API](https://cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/api-platform/system-administration/manage-users-roles). You must have the `apigee.environments.setIamPolicy` permission to call this API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{RESOURCE}} with a real resource.

endpoints.name: POST /v1/{resource}:testIamPermissions
endpoints.description: Tests the permissions of a user on an environment, and returns a subset of permissions that the user has on the environment. If the environment does not exist, an empty permission set is returned (a NOT_FOUND error is not returned).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{GOOGLEAPIS_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your GOOGLEAPIS ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{RESOURCE}} with a real resource.
